content.problemStatement:
Alice has recently learned in her economics class that the market is said to be in equilibrium when the supply is equal to the demand.

Alice has market data for NNN time points in the form of two integer arrays SSS and DDD. Here, SiS_iSi​ denotes the supply at the ithi^{th}ith point of time and DiD_iDi​ denotes the demand at the ithi^{th}ith point of time, for each 1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N.

Help Alice in finding out the number of time points at which the market was in equilibrium.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain an integer TTT — the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
Each test case contains three lines of input.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN, the length of the arrays SSS and DDD.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers S1,S2,…,SNS_1, S_2, \ldots , S_NS1​,S2​,…,SN​.
The third line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers D1,D2,…,DND_1, D_2, \ldots , D_ND1​,D2​,…,DN​.


Output Format
For each test case, output the number of time points at which the market was in equilibrium.
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case:
- A line with integer N, the length of the arrays.
- A line with N space‑separated integers S1 … SN (supply values).
- A line with N space‑separated integers D1 … DN (demand values).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the number of indices i (1 ≤ i ≤ N) such that Si = Di.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: equality at positions 3 and 4, so answer is 2.\nTest case 2: equality only at position 1, so answer is 1.

solution.approach: For each test case we read the two arrays. We initialise a counter to zero and iterate i from 0 to N‑1. Whenever the i‑th element of the supply array equals the i‑th element of the demand array we increment the counter. After the loop we output the counter. This is optimal because every element must be inspected at least once.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, i.e., O(T * N) overall.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides the input arrays.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        S = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        D = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        ans = sum(1 for i in range(N) if S[i] == D[i])
        out_lines.append(str(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
